Discussion
This comprehensive population-based study provides detailed Gradea 1 295 66.3 12.5 506 73.7 3.9
and up-to-date data on oral cavity cancer survival in Germany, 2 2492 51.7 3.1 2346 51.7 2.1
relying on data of over 15,000 patients diagnosed with oral cavity
cancer in 1997–2006 from an underlying population of 33 million
people. The according findings indicate an overall five-year age-
standardized relative survival of 54.6%. For women, survival Alla 4095 49.3 2.4 4273 54.0 1.5
generally decreased with age. For men, survival showed a similar
